title: SMTP SSL configuration issue with Proton Mail
symptom: When attempting to send emails via SMTP with Proton Mail, n8n throws an SSL error indicating 'wrong version number'.
root_cause: The error occurs because the SMTP configuration is set to use SSL while the Proton Mail server requires STARTTLS on port 587. This mismatch leads to the SSL routines failing to negotiate a proper connection.
fix_steps: Restart n8n to apply the changes
agent_instruction: Disable SSL for the SMTP configuration by setting N8N_SMTP_SSL to false.
